Now I have the full picture. Let me identify the version conflicts and create the CPM setup:

| Package | Versions Found | Highest |
|---|---|---|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1, 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2, 1.11.4 | 1.13.2 |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | 8.0.1 | 8.0.1 |
| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | 1.15.0 | 1.15.0 |
| xunit | 2.9.3 | 2.9.3 |

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersions>true</ManagePackageVersions>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information. Let me collect the packages and their versions across all projects:

- System.Text.Json: 10.0.1 (Api), 8.0.4 (Worker), 10.0.1 (Shared), 10.0.1 (Tests) → highest: 10.0.1
- Azure.Identity: 1.13.2 (Api), 1.11.4 (Shared) → highest: 1.13.2
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 8.0.1 (Worker) → 8.0.1
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0 (Worker) → 1.15.0


- xunit: 2.9.3 (Tests) → 2.9.3

Now I need to create a centralized package management file at the solution root with all these versions defined, then strip the version attributes from each project's package references.

data.content:
Done. Here's what was created/changed:

**Created** `Directory.Packages.props` with centralized versions (highest wins):
- `Azure.Identity` → **1.13.2** (was 1.11.4 in Shared)
- `System.Text.Json` → **10.0.1** (was 8.0.4 in Worker)
- `Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting` → 8.0.1
- `OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting` → 1.15.0
- `xunit` → 2.9.3

**Updated** all 4 `.csproj` files to remove `Version` attributes from `<PackageReference>` elements.
